title: Executive Order 11435—Designating the Secretary of the Interior to Accept on Behalf of the United States Retrocession by any State of Certain Criminal and Civil Jurisdiction Over Indian Country

flags.justification: A narrow, specific delegation implementing one provision of a 1968 Act; not a broad new grant.

Requires Federal Register publication specifying the jurisdiction retroceded and effective date, plus mandatory consultation with the Attorney General for criminal-jurisdiction acceptance — a real, built-in cross-check.
Single purpose: delegating one specific statutory acceptance authority.
The acceptance process is precisely bounded: publication requirement plus mandatory Attorney General consultation for criminal matters.
A government-to-government jurisdictional transfer mechanism, not a compliance regime with an incentive structure.
A standing administrative acceptance mechanism under a permanent statutory framework, evergreen by nature.
The mandatory Attorney General consultation requirement adds a cross-check rather than removing one.
A federal-state jurisdictional transfer mechanism, not a third-party compliance regime.
A narrow, technical delegation implementing a specific statutory provision; no broad authority claim.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
